(b) (words beginning “and the authority to amend”), (c), (d)).In subsection (b)(1), the text of 40:514(d) is omitted as executed and obsolete.In subsection (b)(4), the words “chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5” are substituted for “the civil-service and classification laws”, and the words “chapter 33 of title 5” are substituted for “the civil-service laws”, because of (, ), the first section of which enacted Title 5, United States Code. In subclause (A), the words “in the United States” are added for clarity. In subclause (B), provisions related to the heads of executive agencies fixing the compensation of personnel outside the continental limits of the United States that were contained in section 404(c)(2) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 are omitted as obsolete. Sections 1202 and 1204 of the Classification Act of 1949 (ch. 782, , 973) repealed the Classification Act of 1923 (ch. 265, ) and all other provisions inconsistent with the 1949 Act. The Classification Act of 1949 was repealed by (, ), the first section of which enacted title 5, United States Code. The Classification Act of 1949 was reenacted as chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5. See especially 5:5102 and 5103.section 7(b) of the Act of September 6, 1966Public Law 89–55480 Stat. 63163 Stat. 97242 Stat. 1488section 8(a) of the Act of September 6, 1966Public Law 89–55480 Stat. 632In subsection (c)(1), the words “Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961 ( et seq.)” are substituted for “section 32(b)(2) of the Surplus Property Act of 1944, as amended” because of section 111(a)(1) and (c) of the Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961 (, ). The words “Foreign Service Buildings Act, 1926” are substituted for “Foreign Service Buildings Act of , as amended” because of section 8 of the Foreign Service Buildings Act (22:299). The words “(including )” are omitted as executed and obsolete.22 U.S.C. 2451Public Law 87–25775 Stat. 538section 295b of title 22May 7, 1926In subsection (c)(2), the words “Secretary of State” are substituted for “Department of State” because of 22:2651.

children.heading: .—Policies Prescribed by the President
children.label: a .—Policies Prescribed by the President
children.text: The President may prescribe policies that the President considers necessary to carry out this chapter. The policies must be consistent with this chapter.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The head of an executive agency that has foreign excess property is responsible for the disposal of the property.

children.label: A
children.text: use the policies prescribed by the President under subsection (a) for guidance; and

children.label: B
children.text: dispose of foreign excess property in a manner that conforms to the foreign policy of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: delegate authority conferred by this chapter to an official in the agency or to the head of another executive agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: authorize successive redelegation of authority conferred by this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: appoint and fix the pay of personnel in the United States, subject to chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5; and

children.label: B
children.text: appoint personnel outside the States of the United States and the District of Columbia, without regard to chapter 33 of title 5.

children.heading: Special Responsibilities of Secretary of State.—
children.label: c Special Responsibilities of Secretary of State.—
A
children.text: to carry out the Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961 ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 2451

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out the Foreign Service Buildings Act, 1926 ( et seq.); and22 U.S.C. 292

children.label: C
children.text: to pay other governmental expenses payable in local currencies.

children.heading: .—Renewal of certain agreements
children.label: 2 .—Renewal of certain agreements
children.text: Except as otherwise directed by the President, the Secretary of State shall continue to perform functions under agreements in effect on , related to the disposal of foreign excess property. The Secretary of State may amend, modify, and renew the agreements. Foreign currencies or credits the Secretary of State acquires under the agreements shall be administered in accordance with procedures that the Secretary of the Treasury may establish. Foreign currencies or credits reduced to United States currency must be deposited in the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts.July 1, 1949
